title: Aktuelle Operatorarbeit über Tasks, Leases, Checkouts und Sessions verdichten

acceptance.assertion: A read-only projection groups exact tasks, resource leases, linked checkouts, tmux sessions, visible processes and worker records by explicit owner, task, workspace, branch and path bindings; heuristic matches remain separately labelled and never become authority.

The default view is bounded and distinguishes active, blocking, resumable, terminal archived and unknown work, while raw task, lease, checkout, tmux and worker histories remain available through typed drill-down surfaces.
The projection stores no independently mutable lifecycle state and names the authoritative source, observation time, completeness and uncertainty for every grouped item.

Exact child leases remain independently enforceable, but the operator view reports the number and resource classes under each parent operation instead of presenting every child claim as unrelated work.

Historical failed, interrupted and terminal records do not enter the current attention set unless they still have a live exact blocker, unresolved outcome, retained recovery obligation or explicit operator decision requirement.

Tests cover one operation with many component leases, shared repositories with disjoint owners, stale tmux sessions, missing worktrees, terminal tasks with retained evidence, ambiguous heuristic links, truncation and incomplete source snapshots.

A deployed live readback renders the observed high-cardinality state as bounded parent operations with counts and drill-down references, and does not alter any task, lease, checkout, session, process or worker record.

metadata.source_observation: The 2026-07-19 audit observed 8,519 task records, 1,723 attention projections, 110 active leases, 161 Grabowski worktrees and 47 tmux sessions. Exact sources remain authoritative; the defect is operator-level current-work comprehension.
does_not_establish: future action safety from a projection alone
metadata.verification_note: T068 wurde über PR #375 implementiert und nach zwei diffgebundenen Review-Befunden durch PR #377 gehärtet. Beide PRs sind gemergt; der finale Merge-Commit 2eda5eaed5e09dbcb4af68a64e1f9b61df27a9e5 wurde exakt deployt. Runtime-Identität und Integrität sind vollständig gültig, 162/162 Tools stimmen mit dem Deployment-Vertrag überein und ein direkter Loopback-MCP-Aufruf von grabowski_current_work lieferte eine bounded current-Projektion ohne Source-Fehler.
metadata.postmerge_validation_summary: 2839 tests passed, 2 expected skips; full make validate passed on the clean post-merge hardening head before PR #377.

validated_acceptance.source-bound-grouping: Engine and live surface group authoritative task/lease/checkout/workspace bindings separately from heuristic physical observations; heuristics do not grant lifecycle authority.
validated_acceptance.bounded-current-view: Default current view is bounded, paginated and reports active, blocking, resumable, terminal_archived and unknown states; source truncation remains explicit.
validated_acceptance.no-second-truth: The implementation is a read-only derived projection with bounded_source_snapshot scope and stores no independent mutable lifecycle state.
validated_acceptance.lease-aggregation-without-loss: Exact child leases remain enforceable while parent groups expose lease counts and resource-class aggregates; task-owned leases preserve exact task binding even across bounded task windows.
validated_acceptance.attention-relevance: Terminal or closed task lifecycle with a live exact surface is projected blocking; lease-referenced task lifecycles are read exactly within a bound and unresolved lifecycle fails closed.
validated_acceptance.tests: Final clean hardening head passed 2839 tests with 2 expected skips; focused current-work regressions cover lifecycle resolution, truncation and stable pagination identity.
validated_acceptance.live-proof: Exact deployed runtime head 2eda5ea returned grabowski_current_work successfully through the live loopback MCP surface with 162 registered tools, no source errors and explicit attention truncation.
